notes: Gameplay works perfectly out-of-the-box (the fact that it doesn't work on Intel graphics is not a Proton bug); in the menu, the cursor sometimes gets stuck in the middle of the screen, and if another application tries to display something on the screen (e.g. a notification) then the game will freeze for several seconds, though that seems to have improved since Proton 3.7.

notes: Crashes with the message: "No suitable display adapter found"
title: The Witness

notes: Installing Vulkan removed "No suitable display found" error and game started successfully. Disabling D3d11 caused game to crash on start up. With D3d11 enabled and Vulkan installed, the game started successfully, but the transparent screens on symmetry island are invisible, which makes 100% completion of the game impossible. (The glass elevator can probably be ridden by skipping symmetry island, but The Challenge is forever unaccessible.)
title: The Witness

notes: "Geometry quality" has the biggest impact on performance. Runs at 60FPS in 4K (no MSAA) with "Geometry quality" set to "Low" and everything else set to "High".
title: The Witness

notes: Game would not initially run, got a display driver error.  Installed the Mesa Drivers then the DXVK drivers.  It now runs full screen with medium graphics at 720p.  It does pause the game when I enter new areas, I guess for loading of textures.  Then is smooth until I leave for another area.  Only crashed once.  One issue is transparency on some tiles in the symmetry area.
title: The Witness

notes:
I needed to set the launch command in steam to PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%

The game runs really well, but I got a bit stuck and it turned out rendering is a little broken on my setup when it comes to texture transparency.

When multisampling is disabled, texture transparency doesn't work so some clues are impossible to see.  Turning on 2xAA multisampling (or any level) makes it work.  See a comparison https://imgur.com/a/coLGwIg (I first thought it was just the graphic style for ages - reflections in water always have this appearance for me but that's not a problem).
title: The Witness

notes: Works great out of the box. 60fps at 1080p - steady - with max settings. Steam Link to Samsung Smart Tv only sends audio, then disconnects.
title: The Witness

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2070 with Max-Q Design
notes:
Completed entire game using the whitelisted Proton (3.7-8). It would lock up for 5-10 seconds when I changed the volume, presumably due to the on-screen notification from my window manager, and in menus my mouse would be locked to the center of the screen (so I would have to navigate them by keyboard). Had two hard crashes toward the end of the game, but I was able to reload and continue past them.

I briefly tried it with 4.11-1 after I beat the game, and the mouse issue in menu was fixed, and the pauses on volume change were much shorter. For anyone playing the game, it might be worth overriding the default Proton option to see if a newer release handles it better. Nevertheless, it is still entirely playable in 3.7-8.
title: The Witness

notes: PROTON_USE_WINED3D was necessary to get most textures to load. There are definitely still issues with some textures and transparency. Graphical issues didn't interfere with completing the first area, but I expect some later puzzles would be affected.
title: The Witness

notes: Everything works fine out of the box.
title: The Witness

notes: FPS between 70 and 144 capped on High settings. No issues.
title: The Witness

notes: Run this game on my other GPU (AMD Radeon R7 M265) via DRI_PRIME. I can either run it without D3D11, in which case it runs fine in most places, but crashes to desktop in specific areas, or I can run with D3D11, in which case it doesn't crash, but runs at very low FPS in places, and some of the meshes and textures bug out, making part of the game very hard to complete. In either scenario disabling esync is helpful. Neither is ideal, but the game is playable if I switch between the two as and when and am very, very patient.
title: The Witness

notes: Completed the game without any problems.
title: The Witness
